questions.question: Explain the difference between chdir() and fchdir() system calls.
keyPoints: chdir() takes a pathname as argument
fchdir() takes a file descriptor as argument
fchdir() is more efficient when directory is already open
fchdir() avoids pathname resolution

questions.question: What are device special files in Unix? Differentiate between character and block devices.
keyPoints: Device special files provide access to hardware devices
Character devices transfer data character by character (unbuffered)
Block devices transfer data in fixed-size blocks (buffered)
Examples: terminals, keyboards (character); disks, CDs (block)

questions.question: Explain the purpose of major and minor device numbers.
keyPoints: Major number identifies the device driver
Minor number identifies the specific device instance
Together they uniquely identify a device
Used in mknod() and stored in stat structure (st_dev, st_rdev)

questions.question: Write the syntax and return values for getcwd() function.
keyPoints: Syntax: char *getcwd(char *buf, size_t size)
Returns pointer to buffer on success
Returns NULL on failure with errno set
Common error: ERANGE when buffer is too small

questions.question: How does mknod() system call work? Explain its parameters.
keyPoints: mknod() creates a special file (device file, FIFO, or socket)
Parameters: path (filename), mode (file type + permissions), dev (device numbers)
Returns 0 on success, -1 on failure
Only privileged users can create device files (except FIFOs)

questions.question: Explain fchdir() system call in detail with its syntax, parameters, return values, and error conditions.
keyPoints: Syntax: int fchdir(int fd)
fd must refer to an open directory
Returns 0 on success, -1 on failure
Error conditions: EBADF (invalid fd), ENOTDIR (not a directory), EINVAL (unsupported)
Advantages over chdir() - works with O_CLOEXEC directories, avoids path resolution

questions.question: Explain how device special files are represented in Unix/Linux. Discuss the stat structure fields for devices and how to create device files.
keyPoints: Device files are in /dev directory
Two types: character special and block special files
stat structure: st_dev (device ID), st_rdev (real device for specials)
makedev(major, minor) creates device number
mknod() creates device files: mknod(path, S_IFBLK|S_IRWXU|major|minor, makedev(major,minor))
Access through open(), read(), write() system calls
